name: Junsu
training_background: Experienced in both weight training and running; regularly runs 10 km and half marathon distances.
schedule_constraint: Can train up to 6 days per week, up to 90 minutes per session.
preferences: Wants to keep both lifting and running; enjoys structured training; does not want to give up lower-body training.
dislikes: Does not want a plan that sacrifices either lifting or running completely.
must_reflect_constraints: Hypertrophy must be the first priority.
Running should be placed at a maintenance or support level.
Lower-body lifting and running fatigue should not conflict.
dangerous_plan_examples: Intervals or long runs the day after hard lower-body lifting.
Running volume high enough to interfere with hypertrophy recovery.

name: Soyeon
training_background: Almost no exercise experience; very underweight; carries a heavy bag regularly.
primary_goal: Build basic strength and muscle mass
schedule_constraint: 3 days per week, 45–60 minutes per session.
preferences: Wants to start gym training safely.
dislikes: Should avoid overly intense or intimidating programs.
must_reflect_constraints: The plan must be fully beginner-friendly.
Exercise selection should consider lower-back discomfort.
The focus should remain on muscle gain and basic fitness, not extra conditioning.
dangerous_plan_examples: Aggressive heavy axial loading from the start.
Adding running and pushing total fatigue too high.

name: Minseo
secondary_goal: Improve lower-body and general fitness without worsening pain
schedule_constraint: 3–4 days per week, around 60 minutes per session.
preferences: Wants to keep exercising but cautiously.
must_reflect_constraints: Lower-body exercise selection should be knee-aware.
Lower-body work should be modified, not deleted.
dangerous_plan_examples: Jumping, impact-heavy running, repeated deep knee flexion.
High-volume lower-body work that ignores pain.
